response.justification: The candidate is a strong generalist engineer with ~18 years of experience, staff-level titles, and genuine recent exposure to agentic tooling (MCP code generators, AI-agent infrastructure at re:cinq). However, the role requires 8+ years building production AI-driven solutions, and his AI-specific experience amounts to roughly six months. There is no evidence of RAG architectures, vector databases, LLM evaluation pipelines, or multi-agent frameworks like LangGraph/CrewAI, which are core to this position. His depth is in web/full-stack, low-latency systems, and testing craftsmanship rather than applied GenAI delivery.
strengths: 18+ years of broad, production-grade software engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems
Recent hands-on MCP and agentic-workflow tooling experience at re:cinq, plus cloud exposure (AWS CDK, Kubernetes)
Proven mentorship and stakeholder-facing skills via workshops, courses, and conference talks — relevant to forward-deployed work
concerns: Falls far short of the required 8+ years building AI-driven solutions (~6 months of directly relevant experience)
No demonstrated experience with RAG, vector databases, LLM evaluation/observability pipelines, or multi-agent frameworks
No experience shown transitioning AI prototypes to production or integrating AI products into enterprise customer infrastructure

key_factors.factor: Missing RAG, vector DB, and evaluation-pipeline expertise
Strong general engineering seniority and customer-facing mentorship
